metadata.audience: intermediate backend engineers with some microservices experience

chapters.summary: Introduce the concepts of microservices and event-driven architectures, highlighting their benefits and challenges.

subsections.title: What are Microservices?
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain the microservices architectural style, focusing on its core principles and advantages over monolithic applications.
subsections.brief: Define microservices architecture and discuss its key characteristics.

subsections.generator_prompt: Describe event-driven architecture, emphasizing its asynchronous nature and the role of events in communication.
subsections.title: Understanding Event-Driven Architecture
subsections.brief: Explain the principles of event-driven architecture and how it differs from request/response patterns.

subsections.generator_prompt: Outline the benefits (e.g., scalability, resilience) and challenges (e.g., complexity, consistency) of event-driven microservices.
subsections.brief: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of combining microservices with event-driven architecture.
subsections.title: Benefits and Challenges of Event-Driven Microservices

subsections.brief: Define Apache Kafka and explain its key features and capabilities.
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain what Apache Kafka is, highlighting its capabilities as a distributed streaming platform and message broker.
subsections.title: What is Apache Kafka?
Kafka Architecture: Topics, Partitions, and Brokers
subsections.brief: Describe the fundamental components of Kafka architecture.
subsections.generator_prompt: Describe Kafka's architecture, focusing on topics, partitions, brokers, and their roles in data storage and distribution.

subsections.title: Producers and Consumers in Kafka
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain how producers write data to Kafka and how consumers read data from Kafka, detailing the interaction flow.
subsections.brief: Explain how producers and consumers interact with Kafka.

chapters.summary: Introduce Apache Kafka as a distributed streaming platform and its role in event-driven microservices.

chapters.chapter_title: Setting Up a Kafka Development Environment
chapters.summary: Guide readers through setting up a local Kafka development environment.

subsections.brief: Provide step-by-step instructions for installing Kafka and Zookeeper.
subsections.generator_prompt: Provide detailed, step-by-step instructions on how to install Kafka and Zookeeper on a local machine.
subsections.title: Installing Kafka and Zookeeper
Configuring Kafka Brokers
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain the essential configurations for Kafka brokers, such as broker ID, listeners, and log directories.
subsections.brief: Explain the essential Kafka broker configurations.

subsections.generator_prompt: Guide readers through testing the Kafka installation by producing and consuming messages to a test topic.
subsections.title: Testing the Kafka Installation
subsections.brief: Guide readers through testing the Kafka setup.

chapters.summary: Explain the importance of event schemas and how to design them effectively.

subsections.brief: Explain why event schemas are crucial for event-driven systems.
subsections.title: Importance of Event Schemas
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain the importance of event schemas for ensuring data consistency and compatibility in event-driven systems.

subsections.title: Schema Evolution Strategies
subsections.brief: Discuss different strategies for evolving event schemas over time.
subsections.generator_prompt: Discuss different strategies for evolving event schemas, such as adding optional fields, using default values, and schema versioning.
Introduce Apache Avro as a schema definition language and explain how it can be used to define and manage event schemas.
subsections.title: Using Avro for Event Schemas
subsections.brief: Introduce Apache Avro as a schema definition language.

chapters.summary: Guide readers through implementing Kafka producers and consumers using a programming language (e.g., Java or Python).

subsections.brief: Explain how to configure Kafka producers.
subsections.title: Configuring Kafka Producers
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain how to configure Kafka producers, including setting properties like bootstrap servers, serializers, and acknowledgments.

subsections.brief: Demonstrate how to write data to Kafka topics using a producer.
subsections.generator_prompt: Demonstrate how to write data to Kafka topics using a producer, including code examples in a chosen programming language.
subsections.title: Writing Data to Kafka Topics

subsections.brief: Explain how to configure Kafka consumers.
subsections.title: Configuring Kafka Consumers
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain how to configure Kafka consumers, including setting properties like bootstrap servers, deserializers, and group IDs.
Demonstrate how to read data from Kafka topics using a consumer, including code examples in a chosen programming language.
subsections.title: Reading Data from Kafka Topics
subsections.brief: Demonstrate how to read data from Kafka topics using a consumer.

chapters.summary: Guide readers through building event-driven microservices using Kafka.

subsections.title: Identifying Microservice Boundaries
subsections.brief: Discuss how to identify appropriate boundaries for microservices.
subsections.generator_prompt: Discuss how to identify appropriate boundaries for microservices based on domain-driven design principles.

subsections.title: Implementing Microservice Communication with Kafka
subsections.brief: Explain how to implement communication between microservices using Kafka.
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain how to implement communication between microservices using Kafka topics and events, focusing on asynchronous communication patterns.

subsections.title: Handling Data Consistency
subsections.brief: Discuss strategies for handling data consistency in event-driven microservices.
subsections.generator_prompt: Discuss strategies for handling data consistency in event-driven microservices, such as using eventual consistency and compensating transactions.

subsections.title: Introduction to Kafka Streams
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain the purpose and benefits of Kafka Streams as a library for building real-time data processing applications on top of Kafka.
subsections.brief: Explain the purpose and benefits of Kafka Streams.
Guide readers through building stream processing applications with Kafka Streams.
subsections.title: Building Stream Processing Applications
subsections.generator_prompt: Guide readers through building stream processing applications with Kafka Streams, including defining topologies, processing events, and writing results to Kafka topics.

subsections.title: Stateful Stream Processing
subsections.generator_prompt: Discuss stateful stream processing with Kafka Streams, including managing state, handling fault tolerance, and performing aggregations.
subsections.brief: Discuss stateful stream processing with Kafka Streams.

chapters.chapter_title: Kafka Streams for Real-Time Data Processing
chapters.summary: Introduce Kafka Streams as a library for building real-time data processing applications.

subsections.generator_prompt: Explain how to configure authentication and authorization in Kafka using mechanisms like SASL/PLAIN, SASL/SCRAM, and ACLs.
subsections.title: Authentication and Authorization
subsections.brief: Explain how to configure authentication and authorization in Kafka.

subsections.title: Encryption with TLS/SSL
subsections.brief: Explain how to encrypt communication between Kafka components using TLS/SSL.
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain how to encrypt communication between Kafka components using TLS/SSL, including configuring brokers, producers, and consumers for secure communication.

subsections.brief: Discuss options for encrypting data at rest in Kafka.
subsections.generator_prompt: Discuss options for encrypting data at rest in Kafka, such as using disk encryption or Kafka's built-in data encryption features.
subsections.title: Data Encryption at Rest

chapters.summary: Explain how to secure Kafka clusters using authentication, authorization, and encryption.
Discuss how to monitor and observe Kafka clusters and event-driven microservices.

subsections.brief: Explain how to monitor Kafka brokers using metrics and tools.
subsections.title: Monitoring Kafka Brokers
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain how to monitor Kafka brokers using metrics like CPU usage, memory usage, network traffic, and JMX metrics, and tools like Prometheus and Grafana.

subsections.brief: Explain how to monitor Kafka producers and consumers.
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain how to monitor Kafka producers and consumers using metrics like message rates, latency, and error rates, and tools like Kafka Exporter.
subsections.title: Monitoring Producers and Consumers

subsections.brief: Introduce the concept of distributed tracing and how it can be used to trace events across microservices.
subsections.title: Distributed Tracing
subsections.generator_prompt: Introduce the concept of distributed tracing and how it can be used to trace events across microservices using tools like Jaeger and Zipkin.
Discuss best practices for managing event schemas, such as using a schema registry, versioning schemas, and ensuring backward compatibility.
subsections.brief: Discuss best practices for managing event schemas.
subsections.title: Schema Management Best Practices
Error Handling and Fault Tolerance
subsections.brief: Explain best practices for handling errors and ensuring fault tolerance in event-driven microservices.
subsections.generator_prompt: Explain best practices for handling errors and ensuring fault tolerance in event-driven microservices, such as using retry mechanisms, dead-letter queues, and circuit breakers.
Discuss techniques for optimizing the performance of Kafka and event-driven microservices, such as tuning Kafka configurations, optimizing producer and consumer code, and using compression.
subsections.brief: Discuss techniques for optimizing the performance of Kafka and event-driven microservices.
subsections.title: Performance Optimization

chapters.chapter_title: Best Practices for Event-Driven Microservices with Kafka
chapters.summary: Summarize best practices for designing, implementing, and operating event-driven microservices with Kafka.
Present case studies and real-world examples of event-driven microservices architectures using Kafka.

subsections.title: E-commerce Platform
subsections.brief: Describe how an e-commerce platform can leverage event-driven microservices with Kafka.
subsections.generator_prompt: Describe how an e-commerce platform can leverage event-driven microservices with Kafka for order processing, inventory management, and customer notifications.

subsections.title: Financial Services Application
subsections.brief: Describe how a financial services application can use event-driven microservices with Kafka.
subsections.generator_prompt: Describe how a financial services application can use event-driven microservices with Kafka for transaction processing, fraud detection, and real-time analytics.

Summarize the key takeaways from the book and discuss future trends in event-driven microservices with Kafka, such as the adoption of cloud-native technologies and the use of AI/ML for event processing.
subsections.title: Conclusion and Future Trends
subsections.brief: Summarize the key takeaways and discuss future trends in event-driven microservices with Kafka.

title: Event-Driven Microservices with Kafka
subtitle: Designing Resilient, Scalable Systems for the Real World
